title: Hadrolaelia × lilacina
taxonomy: Hadrolaelia × lilacina is classified within the family Orchidaceae and is a hybrid orchid. It belongs to the genus Hadrolaelia and is often confused with other orchids due to its unique characteristics. The classification of Hadrolaelia × lilacina is further broken down into the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Cymbidieae. As a hybrid orchid, its classification is a combination of its parent species. This classification is used to identify and distinguish Hadrolaelia × lilacina from other orchid species.

intro: Hadrolaelia × lilacina, commonly known as the lilac hadrolaelia, is a rare orchid species native to Brazil. The lilac hadrolaelia has been found to possess various health benefits, including its ability to exhibit antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
